skill.name: swift-concurrency-expert
skill.description: Review and fix Swift Concurrency issues in Swift 6.2+ projects using actor isolation, Sendable safety, and minimal code changes.
skill.summary: Guides Swift 6.2+ concurrency reviews, compiler fixes, and safer async code migrations.
skill.author: Dimillian

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and network indicators. Manual review found markdown documentation, Swift code examples, and a single source URL, with no executable code or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Static command detections are markdown false positives
low_findings.description: Backticks and fenced blocks are used for Swift identifiers and examples, not Ruby or shell execution. No runnable script file or command invocation was found.
low_findings.recommendation: No security remediation is required. Keep examples clearly fenced as Swift documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is plain markdown containing Swift annotations, Swift keywords, and code examples. There is no file with executable Ruby, shell, or process-spawning behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography detections are semantic false positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss data-race safety and migration tooling. They do not contain hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.recommendation: No cryptography remediation is needed because the skill does not implement cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found concurrency documentation at these locations, not calls to weak algorithms. The signal appears to come from substring matching rather than security semantics.

low_findings.title: External URL is a source citation only
low_findings.description: The only URL appears in reference material as a source link. It is not fetched, executed, or used to transmit data.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep the citation if useful, or replace it with an official Swift source for stronger editorial quality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is isolated in a Source section of a markdown file. No code path performs network access or exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Swift 6.2 concurrency errors can slow migration and create confusing isolation decisions. This skill guides focused fixes for actors, Sendable, SwiftUI, and approachable concurrency.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Swift Concurrency diagnostics and identifies the affected actor context.
Suggests minimal fixes using MainActor, nonisolated, actor, Sendable, and isolated conformance patterns.
Explains Swift 6.2 approachable concurrency and main-actor-by-default behavior.
Guides SwiftUI concurrency fixes for Sendable closures and UI-bound state.
Helps decide when to offload expensive async work with concurrent functions.
limitations: Does not run the Swift compiler or verify project build settings by itself.
Does not replace human review for thread-safety claims using unchecked Sendable.
Focuses on Swift 6.2+ guidance and may not fit older language modes.
Cannot guarantee performance improvements without profiling real app behavior.

use_cases.title: Fix Migration Errors
use_cases.description: Resolve Swift 6.2 compiler diagnostics while preserving existing UI and model behavior.

use_cases.title: Review SwiftUI Isolation
use_cases.description: Check MainActor boundaries, Sendable closures, and async task structure in SwiftUI views.

use_cases.title: Plan Concurrency Adoption
use_cases.description: Choose safe project settings and migration patterns before enabling stricter concurrency checks.

prompt_templates.title: Explain This Error
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this Swift Concurrency compiler error in simple terms and identify the actor isolation issue: [paste diagnostic and related code].

prompt_templates.title: Suggest Minimal Fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Swift file for Swift 6.2 concurrency issues. Suggest the smallest safe edits and explain each tradeoff.

prompt_templates.title: Audit SwiftUI State
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this SwiftUI view for MainActor, Sendable closure, and Task usage problems. Prioritize fixes that preserve UI behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Swift 6.2 concurrency migration plan for this module. Include settings, likely diagnostics, review order, and risky patterns.

output_examples.input: A compiler error says a main actor property crosses into nonisolated code.
output: Identifies the current actor context and the nonisolated boundary.
Recommends isolated conformance or MainActor annotation when the code is UI-bound.
Explains when nonisolated is unsafe because shared mutable state may cross actors.

output_examples.input: A SwiftUI closure cannot access a view property because the closure is Sendable.
output: Explains why sending self into the closure can violate isolation.
Suggests copying the needed value before the closure when possible.
Notes when model restructuring is better than adding broad annotations.

output_examples.input: A team wants to enable Swift 6.2 approachable concurrency for an app target.
output: Lists areas that still need review, including expensive work and shared state.
Recommends validating changes with compiler diagnostics and targeted tests.

best_practices: Paste exact compiler diagnostics with the smallest related Swift code sample.
State whether the code is UI-bound, background work, or shared mutable state.
Ask for minimal behavior-preserving edits before broader architectural changes.
anti_patterns: Do not add unchecked Sendable without proving thread safety.
Do not mark code nonisolated only to silence a compiler error.
Do not keep CPU-heavy work on the main actor without measuring impact.

faq.question: What Swift versions does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Swift 6.2+ concurrency behavior, including approachable concurrency and main-actor-by-default settings.

faq.question: Can it fix compiler errors automatically?
faq.answer: It can suggest edits and explain them, but the host tool must apply and verify the code changes.

faq.question: Does it work with SwiftUI?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for SwiftUI actor isolation, Sendable closures, Task usage, and UI-bound state.

faq.question: Will it recommend unchecked Sendable?
faq.answer: Only as a last resort when thread safety can be proven. Safer value types and actor isolation are preferred.

faq.question: Can it choose project concurrency settings?
faq.answer: It can explain setting tradeoffs and review likely effects. You still need to verify settings in Xcode or SwiftPM.

faq.question: Does it perform network or filesystem actions?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation and guidance only, with no executable scripts or runtime network behavior.
